mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly non-farm payrolls expand the corporate revenue base and reduce recession risk, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS stands at 131017.0 indicating robust labor expansion
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative when PAYEMS month-over-month change exceeds 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.5% elevates discount rates and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent behavior where industrial production declines while consumer credit expands signals demand-pull inflation without supply support, forcing monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.07 shows supply weakness while RSAFS at 387789.0 shows credit expansion
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when INDPRO drops more than 1% month-over-month and RSAFS rises more than 2% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 600,000 units trigger construction multiplier effects that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when HOUST consistently exceeds 600,000 for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the aggregate corporate earnings base, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions cascade through equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13444.3 indicates positive economic output
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative or flat when GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth for two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and impending recessionary earnings cuts, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.2% confirms severe labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive when UNRATE crosses above 5.5% and stays elevated for at least one month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the CPI index and the federal funds rate indicate an active inflation-fighting policy stance that increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns when both CPI and FEDFUNDS rise month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounding above 95 indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization improvement, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative when INDPRO consistently exceeds 95 for two consecutive months.
